message:
refactor(session_restore): deep cx threading through restore_with_cx + detect_and_restore_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Eighth caller-migration tick in the strangler-fig phase. Closes the
last of the high-level `_with_cx` fns that still delegated to
legacy methods.

`restore_with_cx` rewritten from pre-flight delegate to a fully
cx-threaded multi-step pipeline. Every subsystem call now routes
through its cx-first sibling:

- `LayoutRestorer::restore_with_cx` (tick 93)
- `ScrollbackInjector::inject_with_cx` (tick 94)
- per-pane banner: `wezterm.send_text_with_cx` (tick 44 trait)
- `ProcessLauncher::execute_cx` (pre-existing)

Per-step `cx.checkpoint()` seams also gate each stage so a
cancelled caller can bail between:
- topology parse → layout restore
- layout → scrollback injection
- scrollback → banner send
- banner send → bookkeeping + process relaunch

`detect_and_restore_with_cx` also upgraded:
- `wezterm.list_panes()` → `list_panes_with_cx(cx)` for the
  WezTerm reachability check.

Legacy `restore` + `detect_and_restore` preserved unchanged.

Existing regression test `session_restorer_restore_with_cx_matches_legacy`
(tick 96) pins shape parity: the deepened cx-first path must produce
the same RestoreSummary as the legacy path on a single-pane clean
restore.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 82/82
session_restore tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
